name: rcsb-utils-multiproc

metadata.name: rcsb.utils.multiproc
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: Apache Software License

documentation:
# RCSB Python Multiprocessing Utility Classes

[![Build Status](https://dev.azure.com/rcsb/RCSB%20PDB%20Python%20Projects/_apis/build/status/rcsb.py-rcsb_utils_multiproc?branchName=master)](https://dev.azure.com/rcsb/RCSB%20PDB%20Python%20Projects/_build/latest?definitionId=7&branchName=master)

## Introduction

This module contains a collection of utility classes for multiprocessing operations.

### Installation

Download the library source software from the project repository:

```bash

git clone --recurse-submodules https://github.com/rcsb/py-rcsb_utils_multiproc.git

```

Optionally, run test suite (Python versions 2.7, 3.6, and 3.7) using
[setuptools](https://setuptools.readthedocs.io/en/latest/) or
[tox](http://tox.readthedocs.io/en/latest/example/platform.html):

```bash
python setup.py test

or simply run

tox
```

Installation is via the program [pip](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/pip).

```bash
pip install rcsb.utils.multiproc

or in the local repository:

pip install .
```

modules.name: rcsb.utils.multiproc.MultiProcUtil
documentation: Multiprocessing execution wrapper supporting tasks with list of inputs and a variable number of output lists.
File:    MultiProcUtil.py
Author:  jdw
Date:    3-Nov-2014
Version: 0.001
Updates:
9-Nov-2014  extend API to include variable number of result lists computed by each worker.
11-Nov-2014 extend API with additional options and working directory configuration
21-Apr-2015 jdw change the termination and reaping protocol.
9-Oct-2017 jdw add chunkSize option such that the input dataList to provide more granular distribution
data among the works.  Defaults to numProc if unspecified.
27-Mar-2018 jdw add check for empty input

classes.name: rcsb.utils.multiproc.MultiProcUtil.MultiProcWorker
documentation:
Multi-processing working method wrapper --

Worker method must support the following prototype -

sucessList,resultList,diagList=workerFunc(runList=nextList,procName, optionsD, workingDir)

methods.name: rcsb.utils.multiproc.MultiProcUtil.MultiProcUtil.setOptions
documentation: A dictionary of options that is passed as an argument to the worker function

methods.name: rcsb.utils.multiproc.MultiProcUtil.MultiProcUtil.setWorkingDir
documentation: A working directory option that is passed as an argument to the worker function.

methods.name: rcsb.utils.multiproc.MultiProcUtil.MultiProcUtil.set
documentation:
WorkerObject is the instance of object with method named workerMethod()

Worker method must support the following prototype -

sucessList,resultList,diagList=workerFunc(runList=nextList, procName, optionsD, workingDir)

methods.name: rcsb.utils.multiproc.MultiProcUtil.MultiProcUtil.runMulti
documentation:
Start 'numProc' worker methods consuming the input dataList -

Divide the dataList into sublists/chunks of size 'chunkSize'
if chunkSize <= 0 use chunkSize = numProc

Returns,   successFlag true|false
           failList (data from the inut list that was not successfully processed)
           resultLists[numResults] --  numResults result lists
           diagList --  unique list of diagnostics --

methods.name: rcsb.utils.multiproc.MultiProcUtil.MultiProcUtil.__isHashable
documentation: Test if the input value is hashable

methods.name: rcsb.utils.multiproc.MultiProcUtil.MultiProcUtil.__diffList
documentation: List difference -  elements in l1 not in l2

methods.name: rcsb.utils.multiproc.MultiProcPoolUtil.MultiProcPoolUtil.runMulti
documentation:
Start  a pool of 'numProc' worker methods consuming the input dataList -

Divide the dataList into sublists/chunks of size 'chunkSize'
if chunkSize <= 0 use chunkSize = numProc

sucessList,resultList,diagList=workerFunc(runList=nextList, procName, optionsD, workingDir)

Returns,   successFlag true|false
           failList (data from the inut list that was not successfully processed)
           resultLists[numResults] --  numResults result lists
           diagList --  unique list of diagnostics --

Start  a pool of 'numProc' worker methods consuming the input dataList -

Divide the dataList into sublists/chunks of size 'chunkSize'
if chunkSize <= 0 use chunkSize = numProc

sucessList,resultList,diagList=workerFunc(runList=nextList, procName, optionsD, workingDir)

Returns,   successFlag true|false

modules.name: rcsb.utils.multiproc.MultiProcLogging
documentation: Multiprocessing logging queue handler and listener.
File:    MultiProcLogging.py
Author:  jdw
Date:    6-Apr-2018
Version: 0.001
Updates:

methods.name: rcsb.utils.multiproc.MultiProcLogging.MultiProcLogging.__init__
documentation:
Current logging instance or None - alternative format and level to be used within the bounded context.

Redirect log requests to an multi-proc queue and a listener that
redirects the request to handers bound to the input logger instance.

classes.name: rcsb.utils.multiproc.MultiProcLogging.MultiProcLogQueueHandler

methods.name: rcsb.utils.multiproc.MultiProcLogging.MultiProcLogQueueHandler.__init__
documentation: Initialise an instance, using the passed queue.

methods.name: rcsb.utils.multiproc.MultiProcLogging.MultiProcLogQueueHandler.enqueue
documentation:
Enqueue a record.

The base implementation uses put_nowait. You may want to override
this method if you want to use blocking, timeouts or custom queue
implementations.

methods.name: rcsb.utils.multiproc.MultiProcLogging.MultiProcLogQueueHandler.prepare
documentation:
Prepares a record for queuing. The object returned by this method is
enqueued.

The base implementation formats the record to merge the message
and arguments, and removes unpickleable items from the record
in-place.

You might want to override this method if you want to convert
the record to a dict or JSON string, or send a modified copy
of the record while leaving the original intact.

methods.name: rcsb.utils.multiproc.MultiProcLogging.MultiProcLogQueueHandler.emit
documentation:
Emit a record.

Writes the LogRecord to the queue, preparing it for pickling first.

This logging handler sends events to a queue. Typically, it would be used together
with a multiprocessing Queue to centralise logging to file in one process
(in a multi-process application), so as to avoid file write contention
between processes.
The following handler and listener classes are provided for Py2 compatibility
and come from the Python 2 distribution.  They are mimimally adapted here.

classes.name: rcsb.utils.multiproc.MultiProcLogging.MultiProcLogQueueListener

methods.name: rcsb.utils.multiproc.MultiProcLogging.MultiProcLogQueueListener.__init__
documentation: Initialise an instance with the specified queue and handlers.

methods.name: rcsb.utils.multiproc.MultiProcLogging.MultiProcLogQueueListener.dequeue
documentation:
Dequeue a record and return it, optionally blocking.

The base implementation uses get. You may want to override this method
if you want to use timeouts or work with custom queue implementations.

methods.name: rcsb.utils.multiproc.MultiProcLogging.MultiProcLogQueueListener.start
documentation:
Start the listener.

This starts up a background thread to monitor the queue for
LogRecords to process.

methods.name: rcsb.utils.multiproc.MultiProcLogging.MultiProcLogQueueListener.prepare
documentation:
Prepare a record for handling.

This method just returns the passed-in record. You may want to
override this method if you need to do any custom marshalling or
manipulation of the record before passing it to the handlers.

methods.name: rcsb.utils.multiproc.MultiProcLogging.MultiProcLogQueueListener.handle
documentation:
Handle a record.

This just loops through the handlers offering them the record
to handle.

methods.name: rcsb.utils.multiproc.MultiProcLogging.MultiProcLogQueueListener._monitor
documentation:
Monitor the queue for records, and ask the handler
to deal with them.

This method runs on a separate, internal thread.
The thread will terminate if it sees a sentinel object in the queue.

methods.name: rcsb.utils.multiproc.MultiProcLogging.MultiProcLogQueueListener.stop
documentation:
Stop the listener.

This asks the thread to terminate, and then waits for it to do so.
Note that if you don't call this before your application exits, there
may be some records still left on the queue, which won't be processed.

This class implements an internal threaded listener which watches for
LogRecords being added to a queue, removes them and passes them to a
list of handlers for processing.
